fields.description: The AB2258 microprocessor development focuses on creating a flexible solution that can be used to provide core processing needs for a wide variety of industrial applications.

fields.description: The interface shall provide a raster controller.
fields.name: Raster Controller

fields.description: The interface shall provide an LC interface display driver.
fields.name: LCD Display Driver

fields.description: The interface shall drive a maximum of 2048x2048 pixels.
fields.name: Maximum Resolution

fields.description: The LCDC consists of two independent controllers, the raster controller and the LCD interface display driver (LIDD) controller. Each controller operates independently from the other and only one of them is active at any given time.
fields.name: Architecture

fields.description: The raster controller handles the synchronous LCD interface. It provides timing and data for constant graphics refresh to a passive display. It supports a wide variety of monochrome and full-color display types and sizes by use of programmable timing controls, a built-in palette, and a gray-scale and serializer. Graphics data is processed and stored in frame buffers. A frame buffer is a contiguous memory block in the system. A built-in DMA engine supplies the graphics data to the raster engine which, in turn, outputs to the external LCD device.
fields.name: Raster Controller

fields.description: The LIDD controller supports the asynchronous LCD interface. It provides full-timing programmability of control signals (CS, WE, OE, ALE) and output data.
fields.name: LIDD Controller
Verify the display of 2048x2048 pixel image
Verify Output Load Capacitance meets requirements
Verify the Setup, Hold and Transition Times

fields.name: mDDR

fields.name: DDR2

fields.description: The interface shall support DDR3.
fields.name: DDR3

fields.description: The interface shall support DDR3L.
fields.name: DDR3L

fields.description:
The GPMC is the unified memory controller used to interface external memory devices such as:

  • Asynchronous SRAM-like memories and ASIC devices
  • Asynchronous page mode and synchronous burst NOR flash
  • NAND flash
fields.name: GPMC

fields.description: The device has a dedicated interface to mDDR(LPDDR), DDR2, DDR3, and DDR3L SDRAM. It supports JEDEC standard compliant mDDR(LPDDR), DDR2, DDR3, and DDR3L SDRAM devices with a 16-bit data path to external SDRAM memory.
fields.name: DDR Memory Interface

fields.description:
About the Datasheet Item Types

In a production instance of Jama, we typically recommend splitting out datasheet item types into their own item types, since sometimes you'll want to track Min/Typ/Max/Unit values, and other times you'll be tracking Package Code or Location, etc. In this trial instance of Jama, we've kept things simple by designating only ONE item type for the datasheet, so that the relationship diagram and explorer tree are easier to understand for users who are brand new to Jama. The rest of this READ ME document will outline the differences between a typical Production Instance of Jama and the Trial Instance that you have in front of you right now.

 

The Production Instance
fields.name: READ ME

fields.description: This microprocessor, based on the ARM Cortex-A8 processor, is enhanced with image, graphics processing, peripherals and industrial interface options such as EtherCAT and PROFIBUS. The devices support high-level operating systems (HLOS), such as Linux® and Android™.
fields.name: Description

fields.description:
Up to 1-GHz ARM® Cortex®-A8 32‑Bit Processor

  • Coprocessor
  • 32KB of L1 Instruction and 32KB of Data Cache
  • 256KB of L2 Cache With Error Correcting Code (ECC)
  • 176KB of On-Chip Boot ROM
  • 64KB of Dedicated RAM
  • Emulation and Debug - JTAG
  • Interrupt Controller (up to 128 Interrupt Requests)


On-Chip Memory (Shared L3 RAM)

  • 64KB of General-Purpose On-Chip Memory Controller (OCMC) RAM
  • Accessible to All Masters
  • Supports Retention for Fast Wakeup


External Memory Interfaces (EMIF)

  • mDDR(LPDDR), DDR2, DDR3, DDR3L Controller:
    • mDDR: 200-MHz Clock (400-MHz Data Rate)
    • DDR2: 266-MHz Clock (532-MHz Data Rate)
    • DDR3: 400-MHz Clock (800-MHz Data Rate)
    • DDR3L: 400-MHz Clock (800-MHz Data Rate)
    • 16-Bit Data Bus
    • 1GB of Total Addressable Space
    • Supports One x16 or Two x8 Memory Device Configurations
    • General-Purpose Memory Controller (GPMC)
    • Flexible 8-Bit and 16-Bit Asynchronous Memory Interface
    • Uses BCH Code to Support 4-, 8-, or 16-Bit ECC
    • Uses Hamming Code to Support 1-Bit ECC
  • Error Locator Module (ELM)
    • Used in Conjunction With the GPMC to Locate Addresses of Data Errors from Syndrome Polynomials Generated Using a BCH Algorithm
    • Supports 4-, 8-, and 16-Bit per 512-Byte Block Error Location Based on BCH Algorithms

fields.description:

  • Home and Industrial Automation
  • Consumer Medical Appliances
  • Printers
  • Smart Toll Systems
  • Connected Vending Machines
  • Weighing Scales
  • Educational Consoles
  • Advanced Toys

fields.description:
Figure 6-7 shows the VDD_MPU_MON connectivity. VDD_MPU_MON connectivity is available only on the ZCZ package.

 


Figure 6-7 VDD_MPU_MON Connectivity
fields.name: VDD_MPU_MON Connections

fields.description:
Figure 6-11 shows the recommended oscillator connections when OSC0 is connected to an LVCMOS square-wave digital clock source. The LVCMOS clock source is connected to the XTALIN terminal. The ground for the LVCMOS clock source and VSS_OSC should be connected directly to the nearest PCB digital ground (VSS). In this mode of operation, the XTALOUT terminal should not be used to source any external components. The printed circuit board design should provide a mechanism to disconnect the XTALOUT terminal from any external components or signal traces that may couple noise into OSC0 via the XTALOUT terminal.

 

The XTALIN terminal has a 15- to 40-kΩ internal pulldown resistor which is enabled when OSC0 is disabled. This internal resistor prevents the XTALIN terminal from floating to an invalid logic level which may increase leakage current through the oscillator input buffer.
fields.name: OSC0 LVCMOS Digital Clock Source

fields.description: To maintain the safe operating range of the internal ESD protection devices, TI recommends limiting the maximum slew rate for powering on the supplies to be less than 1.0E +5 V/s. For instance, as shown in Figure 6-1, TI recommends a value greater than 18 µs for the supply ramp slew for a 1.8-V supply.
fields.name: Power Supply Slew Rate
